More specifically, it falls under the subfamily Opuntioideae and is a member of the genus Neoporteria. The Neoporteria omasensis is native to the Atacama Desert in Chile. The Neoporteria omasensis classification is used to distinguish it from other similar species within the same genus.

intro: Neoporteria omasensis, also known as Omasensea, is a succulent plant species native to the Atacama Desert in Chile, belonging to the Euphorbiaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, and its health benefits include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and skin irritations.
